feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Datasphere & SAC Users and Activities Source Tables
entry.content:
Hi All,

I would like to create usage reports (Activities related to which views from Datasphere are accessed by SAC and the user that viewed the story in SAC and last login date for individual users for both Datasphere and SAC) based on the Users and Activities tables found in Datasphere and SAC under the Security Tab.

 

I would like to avoid having to download the CSVs from the User and Activities tabs completely and rather use the underlying tables where this data is stored directly.

Where can I find the source tables? Using the Database Explorer on Datasphere I have looked under the SYS and DWC_Global schemas so far with no luck.

entry.title: SAP BDC Demo Environment
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Since the BDC trial version is now available, I’d like to create a demo using it. Could you please guide me on how to enable or build the content? Also, is it possible to showcase a demo using standard content, considering that the source systems are not properly maintained? I’m looking for any way to effectively pitch it to clients. I’m exploring options to work exclusively within BDC AI/ML part. The practice source systems are not properly maintained, making it impractical to establish connections with them. However, intelligent applications or insight apps require both source and target systems to be connected in order to enable the content. Are there any materials or documents available that can guide the implementation of Databricks AI/ML on raw data within an SAP BDC environment?

entry.title: Automate dropping of local tables in SAP Datasphere with SQL
entry.content:
Hi,

I am quite new to SAP Datasphere.

I have some task chains scheduled for daily run.

I have a few local tables scheduled for daily deletion as well.

I'd like to have a way to automate this local tables deletion, in case that i have to manually run the task chains again and they need these local tables to be previously deleted. Its is a risk to delete them manually one by one, as i can miss something.

As i understand, i cannot have this being done within a Task Chain nor have such commands as "drop table" using SQL View as this is a data manipulation.

Is there a way to automate the dropping of tables with SQL inside Datasphere?

Or any other option?

Appreciate your help.

entry.title: Text is not visible in lower level of level based hierarchy with compounded key
entry.content:
I have an Analytics model over SAP SD data on ECC

I have created a Customer Sales dimension with level based hierarchy with 3 levels

Customer_Hier1

Customer_Hier2

Customer

Each has a text view connected to it

In the Analytic model both via preview or SAC I can see the hierarchy correct with test on the upper levels but no text on the lower level of Customer

When I display the object with flat hierarchy I do see the customer text

Thanks

Shlomi

entry.title: Is Data extraction from SAP SF-ICM to SAP datasphere in delta possible
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Is delta extraction from SAP SuccessFactors-ICM (Incentive Compensation Management) to SAP Datasphere possible, wanted to pull the tables that starts with "CS_'.

When I tried adding the CS_* tables in the Replication flow of SAP Datasphere, with delta mode as "Initial and Delta" it is getting failed. 

"Initial only" is working fine for these tables, wanted to check whether delta support is available/any approach to pull the data in delta mode.

Can someone help on this. Thanks!

--

KRPK

SAP Datasphere SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management

entry.title: Stack Analytical Models
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Recently the feature to stack Analytical models (AM) has been released.


I already see two benefits:

- Data Access Control on first AM (with options to add more detailed DAC on second AM...) 

- Calculations managed on the first AM

 

I was wondering if there are other benefits/ calculations possible due to this new feature?

Would love to know how others see this feature (filtering, restrictions,..) .

 

Kind regards

entry.title: How to lift BW 7.5 to BW PCE ?
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

As part of SAP campaign for SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), where the customer with SAP BW on-premise can do "lift and shift" their BW seamlessly and integrating to SAP BDC.

Currently I have a customer that need to lift their on-premise BW 7.5 to BW PCE. May I know how it works? Does the customer need other third party tools / installed apps for lift on-premise BW to cloud or it's only simple process as simple as backup the on-premise BW and restore it to BW PCE?

BW (SAP Business Warehouse) , SAP Business Data Cloud , SAP Datasphere

entry.title: SAP Cold Data Management - BW on HANA and ADLS Integration via SAP Datasphere or BDC
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community members, 

We are planning to move the cold data storage from On-prem SAP BW on HANA to cost-effective Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS). So currently we are exploring various options to perform this functionality. So please provide the best and recommended option to achieve this functionality.

fyi, in our landscape we already have On-Prem SAP BW on HANA, SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud applications and also plan to have a BDC tenant subscription.

entry.title: How to Add Hierarchy to SQL View in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’ve created a SQL view in SAP Datasphere and would like to include the G/L Account Hierarchy in the view. I have access to the G/L Account Hierarchy Directory View and G/L Account Hierarchy Node View within Datasphere, but I'm not sure how to properly join or integrate them into my SQL view to reflect the hierarchical structure.

I’ve searched the SAP Community and Help Portal but couldn’t find a clear or conclusive solution.

Has anyone successfully done this or can offer guidance on the best approach?

Thanks in advance for your help! #SAP SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Accessing SAP Datasphere from ABAP System (S/4HANA OP)
entry.content:
Hi,

Our requirement is to access data exposed in SAP Datasphere from an ABAP system (S/4HANA OP) via ODBC. Going through the help Consume Data in Power BI and Other Clients, Tools, and Apps via ODBC/JDBC | SAP Help Portal it is not clear whether the following step is from ABAP system perspective or not. 

  • Install the SAP HANA CLIENT 2.0 (see Download and Install the SAP HANA Client in the SAP HANA Cloud documentation).

Our use case is to read certain data in SAP Datasphere from a Fiori app hosted in the ABAP system. This Fiori app is used by developers internally in SAP. Hence there cannot be a prerequisite that something is installed in every developer's laptop. I might be wrong in my understanding. Hence requesting clarity and guidance here. 

Regards,

Madhura Lobo

entry.title: Issue applying Hierarchy on a non key column in Dimension table in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working in SAP Datasphere and need assistance regarding applying a hierarchy on a non-key attribute in a dimension table.

Objective:
I would like to apply a hierarchy on the ECONOMIC ACTIVITY attribute (which contains NACE codes) in the PARTY dimension table.

My Setup:

  • I have a local table named PARTY, where:

    • PARTY_ID is the primary key

    • ECONOMIC ACTIVITY (technical name: ECNMC_ACTVTY) is a regular attribute (not a key)

  • I have created a separate NACE Code Hierarchy table, with semantic usage set as "Dimension with Hierarchy Directory"

    • The hierarchy is defined using standard parent-child relationships

  • I also created a hierarchy directory dimension to represent the NACE code levels, and associated it with the ECONOMIC ACTIVITY field in the PARTY table via a text/association mapping

In the Analytic Model:

  • The Analytic Model is built on top of a FACT table

  • The FACT table is joined to the PARTY table using RELEVANT BUSINESS PARTNER ID → PARTY_ID

  • The join is working as expected, and the RELEVANT BUSINESS PARTNER ID node exposes dimensions from the PARTY table, including ECONOMIC ACTIVITY

  • However, the hierarchy is only available or shown on RELEVANT BUSINESS PARTNER ID, and not on ECONOMIC ACTIVITY

Question:
Why is the hierarchy not appearing for ECONOMIC ACTIVITY in the Analytic Model? Am I missing a step in exposing or enabling the hierarchy on a non-key attribute? Or is it the case that hierarchies in SAP Datasphere are only supported for primary key fields in dimension tables?

Any clarification or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

 

 

Thanks,

Mayura

entry.title: DS Wave 2025.10 filter variable range - bug
entry.content:
With the newest update you can set defaults for range variables.

However it seems that when using [ ] mistake with sorting pops up

 

 

12 is seen as lower since it starts with a '1' and string wise is lower than 6... But this is not correct for number data types of course. Is this intended behavior?

 

Kind regards

entry.title: Python code error in dataflow
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm very new to python, I hvae a data set with 13 column , once of the called 'gender'. I want to filter the data and result moved to Target table.

However I'm getting below error message called "'method' object is not subscriptable"

Please my code below, unfortunatly we can't debug the code in datasphere

def transform(data):
specific_name = ['Male']
df = data['gender'].isin[('specific_name')]
#data[NV]
return df

Any input would be greatly appreciated

Kind regards

Hari

entry.title: Datasphere - Connection is valid, but not all features are available
entry.content:
Datasphere - Connection is valid, but not all features are available. Data Flows: Cause Timed out while connecting to the source.

On setting up source connetions - Validating connection to on-premises in datasphere and getting above error. Confirmed is reaching onpremise souce database and authenticating

Versions:

dpagent 2.8.0 

OracleLogReader 

Oracle 12.1.0.2 - ECC 6 EHP8

Have tried using ojdbc8.jar & ojdbc7.jar

No errors in logs, set logging level to all and nothing stands out.

Anyone else experience similar?

entry.title: How to get quota values & its corresponding year and month details from SF-ICM(Incentive Management)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am trying to build a report in Datasphere to show quota details, fetching from CS_FixedValue and CS_Variable tables (Successfactors - Incentive management)

I wanted to display the year and month wise quota information. But couldn't find a logic to join the the above tables with CS_Period/CS_PeriodType.

Is there any logic or lookup table that stores the quota information and its corresponding months when it got posted. Thanks!

--KRPK
 SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management  SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Get the error "cannot commit package xxx and earlier packages in the sequence" in replication flow
entry.content: During the initial load using a replication flow with SLT in SAP Datasphere I keep getting the error message "Cannot commit package xxx and earlier packages in the sequence/ Cannot lock packages for buffer / Lock on table DHRDB_PACKAGE failed. This only happens for the bigger tables (like VBAK, VBAP, BSEG, ACDOCA), smaller tables are working fine. Does anyone faced the same problem?

entry.title: Connection issue in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are trying to establish a connection from SAP Datasphere (Test Tenant) to an SAP ABAP Test system. The connection between the Datasphere Dev Tenant and Dev ABAP system is working perfectly — the validation is successful.

However, when attempting to connect the Datasphere Test Tenant to the Test ABAP system, the connection fails with the following error:

"Connection "TEST_CONN1" couldn’t be established.
- Data flows can’t be used because of errors in the connection.
- Replication flows can’t be used because of errors in the connection.
- Remote tables are disabled.

Data Flows: Cause: ABAP connector(Axino) ABAP connection check FAILED: request failed: rc=1, msg="Message: An invalid handle 'RFC_CONNECTION_HANDLE' was passed to the API call\nCode: RFC_INVALID_HANDLE"

Please refer to SAP Note 2849542 for more information.
Replication Flows: Cause: ABAP connector(Axino) ABAP connection check FAILED: request failed: rc=1, msg="Message: An invalid handle 'RFC_CONNECTION_HANDLE' was passed to the API call\nCode: RFC_INVALID_HANDLE"

Please refer to SAP Note 2849542 for more information."

We have already compared and verified the technical user roles in the Test system comparing with the Dev system. Required resources have also been added to the Cloud Connector.

Has anyone else encountered this issue? Any suggestions or solutions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: How to use recursive query in SQL Views in SAP Datasphere?
entry.content: I need to write a query to find the hierarchical data for manager / employee data, but Datasphere throws an error with the 'with' clause while writing cte's. Can anyone please tell me how to use recursive query in SQL Views in SAP Datasphere?

entry.title: Request for Solution: CDS View with Parameters (Key Date) Not Supported in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently integrating a consumption CDS view with SAP Datasphere. This view includes parameters used for Key Date calculations. However, it appears that parameter-based CDS views are currently not supported in Datasphere, and as a result, these views are not visible or accessible within Datasphere.

Current Architecture:

  • Source: SAP S/4HANA CDS Views (with parameters)
  • Data Layer: SAP Datasphere
  • Cloud Storage: Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
  • Reporting Tool: Qlik Sense

Issue Description:

Due to the use of parameters (specifically for Key Date logic), the CDS views are not visible in Datasphere. To work around this, we attempted to create a wrapper CDS view to eliminate parameters by replacing the Key Date with the current system date ($systemDate). However, this approach does not yield the correct results. While it retrieves data until the current date, it fails to calculate key figures as expected based on Key Date logic.

Request for Guidance:

  1. Is there any supported approach in SAP Datasphere for handling CDS views with Key Date parameters?
  2. Are there alternative solutions or best practices in S/4HANA or Datasphere to handle such cases?
  3. Would it be possible to implement the Key Date logic in GCP?
    • If so, what changes or configurations would be required on the S/4HANA side to support this?
  5. Are there any recommended design patterns or tools to replicate the Key Date functionality in an environment where parameterized views are not supported?

This issue is critical and is currently impacting key project deliverables. Your urgent assistance would be highly appreciated.

Please find attached the CDS views in question, including those with parameters. Let us know if you need any additional details.

Thanks,

Sridhar

entry.title: Transformation Mapping IPS Groups to SAP Datasphere Profiles
entry.content:
Hello,

I’m currently working on setting up a transformation mapping between an IPS group and SAP Datasphere.
Here is the transformation code I’m using:

{
                "condition": "$.groups[?(@.display == 'GLB APP SAP IAS_DEV BTP Datasphere DEV TESTSCIM')] != []",
                "constant": {
                    "value": "PROFILE:t.MGQALN:EK_SR_P_WORKSHOP",
                    "display": "EK_SR_P_WORKSHOP",
                    "primary": true
                },
                "targetPath": "$.roles[0]"
            },
            {
                "condition": "$.groups[?(@.display == 'GLB APP SAP IAS_DEV BTP Datasphere DEV TESTSCIM_ADMIN')] != []",
                "constant": {
                    "value": "PROFILE:sap.dwc:Data_Warehouse_Cloud_Administrator",
                    "display": "Data_Warehouse_Cloud_Administrator",
                    "primary": true
                },
                "targetPath": "$.roles[1]"
            }

 

What works with this:

If the user is assigned to one or both groups, they are successfully created in SAP Datasphere.
The corresponding profile (role) is also assigned.

What doesn’t work:

If I remove a group, the associated profile in SAP Datasphere is not removed.



Does anyone have an idea how to solve this?

Best regards,
Andreas
